title: pycnanthemum incanum var. albescens
intro: Pycnanthemum incanum var. albescens, also known as the white panicle mountain mint, is a plant species native to North America, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and minty aroma. The medicinal properties of Pycnanthemum incanum var. albescens include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its ability to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include relief from colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive problems and skin irritations. Traditionally, Pycnanthemum incanum var. albescens has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and can be consumed fresh, dried, or in tincture form.
